video_name: Video_07_Structured Output in LangChain ｜ Generative AI using LangChain ｜ Video 5 ｜ CampusX.mp3
full_text: Hi guys, my name is Nitesh and you are welcome to my YouTube channel In this video, we are going to continue our language chain playlist and today's video is on a very interesting topic and the name of this topic is Structured Output Before starting the video, I would like to give you a little introduction about this topic So, if you have used any LLM or chat GPT software till date then you must have noticed that the biggest feature of LLMs is that you can talk to humans in natural language Right? But what if we can make LLMs talk to other machines or systems For example, I want my LLM to talk to my database or my LLM to talk directly to my APIs So, we are going to learn this in today's video and to watch today's video well, I want to give you a big motivation In the future, when I will teach you about agents which is a big buzzword in today's date then you will find today's topic very useful So, on the whole, very important topic Please make sure you don't skip this and watch this video end to end Now, let's start the video So guys, before starting the video, let's take a recap So, in the previous video, we discussed prompts What are prompts in the language chain So, I told you that prompts are basically inputs that you give to LLMs Right? So, in a way, I can say that in the previous video, we learned to give input to LLMs Now, in today's video, we will learn to work with the output coming from LLMs In fact, in today's video and in the next video, we will learn to process the output coming from LLMs So, that is the main goal of today's video Now, the topic of the video is structured output So, let me explain to you what is structured output Now guys, before understanding structured output, let me tell you what is unstructured output So, if you have talked to any LLM, let's say, you have talked to chat GPT You must have noticed that you send a text input to chat GPT Which you call prompt As soon as this prompt goes to your LLM, your LLM processes that prompt and then generates a response And this response, generally speaking, is also a text Like, if I say what is the capital of India, then I will get a response that New Delhi is the capital of India Right? Now, since the response is in text format, that is why we can call it unstructured output Because there is no structure in text, right? That is why the textual output that you get, you call it unstructured output So, generally, when you are talking to any LLM, then you should accept that the output you are getting is unstructured Right? But, what if we can get some structure from it? What am I saying? Let me explain Let's say you gave this prompt to your LLM Generally, what will happen is that your LLM will give you a response like this In the morning, visit Eiffel Tower In the afternoon, visit a museum In the evening, have dinner Right? Now, since this is text, this is unstructured But, what if our model gives us some kind of output like this? What should it do? That it first of all gives us output in JSON format And that output should be structured in such a way So, in total, three items are being told to you So, they are being stored in three dictionaries In every dictionary, time is told and activity is told Right? You can see In the second dictionary, time and activity is told In the third one, time and activity is told We call this structured output Because there is a structure in it Right? Which is being followed Now, this structured output can be very beneficial I will tell you later where you can use this kind of structured output But for now, let's see this definition Right? Not only in English, we don't need a response We need a response in a data format Right? Like JSON Right? What is the benefit of this? That you can integrate this LLM with other systems Very easily This is the biggest benefit In fact, now what I will do is I will tell you some use cases That where structured outputs can give you a lot of benefit So, I will tell you three use cases But trust me There are more use cases than this So, the first use case is data extraction You are making a system where you have to store the output from LLM somewhere Let's say in a database So, you can use the structured output For example, you are making a job portal Like job.com Now, people upload their resume on your job portal But what you want is that you extract some key information from this resume Like what is the name of the candidate And what was his last company What were his marks on 12th What were marks on 10th What marks were there in college You want to store all this Right? Into a database So, what you can do here is You can use the structured output very easily The candidate will upload the resume So, we will extract all the text from the resume We will send this text to LLM And LLM will extract all this information in JSON format And then we will write code on this JSON information And insert all this information into a database And we will do this for all our candidates Right? So, this can be a very good use case Second use case is If you want to build APIs Suppose you work for Amazon People write reviews of products on Amazon You must have noticed that reviews are very long Right? And they are textual Means they are unstructured What you can do is You can write a code Which will take up a complete review And will generate some key information from it Like what is the topic What is the topic of this review So, if it is a phone review Then in this phone review We talked about the battery We talked about the display We talked about the processor You will extract all those topics After that, what you have told in the reviews You will store it in one place What you have told about the cons You will store it in one place And what is the overall sentiment of that review You will store it in one place Right? And then you can give this information to anyone Right? Since you have made an API using this Using a service like Flask or Fast API Now anyone can take the output of this API in the whole world Right? So, this can be a very good second use case And the third use case is To build agents So, if you remember I have told you about agents in the second video of this playlist I told you that agents are chatbots on steroids Chatbots can only talk to you But agents can also do some work Right? So, I told you that agents need tools to do their work Right? Like we can make a maths based agent So, that agent can have a tool called calculator Which will perform any complex mathematical operation Right? Now if you will tell this agent to find the square root of 2 Then you cannot send this text directly to the calculator Because calculator is expecting numbers So, if you Use structured output on the prompt that agent has And fetch those numbers Right? That you have to find the square root And whose square root? 2 Now if you send these two pieces of information to the calculator Then calculator will do its work and give it to you Right? So, all these tools that you use with agents All of them need structured output They cannot work on textual data Right? So, in the future when I will teach you how to make agents You will remember today's video How we generate structured output from LLM And how we send those structured output to tool And tool executes its work Right? So, these are three very important use cases But more use cases exist than trust And I would recommend you to think about it That where you can use this structured output In fact, I would recommend you to write a good idea in the comments Right? So, if I summarize the discussion till now Structured output is a very powerful technique Basic funda is that till today we were using LLMs To interact with humans Right? Humans used to send messages LLMs used to send messages in reverse And they used to understand each other's point The only problem is that LLMs are sending textual output They are sending unstructured output That's why you were not able to connect LLMs with other systems Other systems like databases Or your APIs We were not able to integrate LLMs with such systems Because LLMs output is unstructured It is textual So, with the help of structured output We gave a structure to output from LLM We gave a data format Now, since we have given this data format Now, LLMs can talk with other systems So, this is the main benefit of having structured output Right? Till now, LLMs were talking with humans Now, LLMs can talk with other machines All because of structured output And that is why I am repeating in this video That this is a very important topic that you should learn Before coding, I would like to clarify one thing That you will get to see two types of LLMs One type of LLMs that can generate structured output by default Like OpenAI GPT models They are trained in such a way that if you talk to them They can generate structured output and give you Right? And at the same time, you will get some LLMs That don't have the capability to generate structured output So, in LLMs, you can work with these two types of models And generate structured output So, those models that can generate structured output You get a function to work with them in LLMs Which is called with structured output With this function, everything gets simplified What can you do? You can use this function and easily tell your LLM Which data format you want to use And your LLM will give you the output in that format Now, there is a way for models that can't generate structured output by default There is a way for them as well And that is called output parsers So, output parsers are classes written in line chain With the help of which you can structure unstructured output of any LLM So, my plan is that in today's video I will work with those LLMs that can give structured output by default Meaning, today's video will revolve around with structured output function We will work with this And in the next video, we will work with those models that can't give structured output And in the next video, we will discuss the concept of output parsers So, in these two videos, you will understand completely How you can generate structured output from any type of LLM So, I hope you understood this important distinction Now, let's talk about with structured output function Now, with structured output function, it is easy to generate structured output In fact, we will use the flow that you have seen so far We will just make a small change So, what happens is that when you invoke your model Which we have seen in the last two videos Before invoking the model, you have to call with structured output function And here you have to tell your data format And that's it That's the only difference Now, there is one thing that you can learn That you can specify data formats in two or three ways In fact, we will see three ways The first way is using typed dictionary Let's discuss about it in a while The second way is Pydantic Those who have worked with fast API might have heard about Pydantic And the third one is JSON Schema So, we will learn to specify data formats in three ways Not only that, I will also tell you when and which method you should use But let's do one thing First, let's generate structured output with the help of typed dictionary Let's understand what is typed dictionary You might not have studied it in Python So, here the definition is written Typed dictionary is a way to define a dictionary in Python Where you specify what keys and values should exist It helps ensure that your dictionary follows a specific structure Till now, you used to make a dictionary in Python With the name of a person So, you used to make a dictionary on the go That this is the name of the person This is the age of the person And then you used to use it But the problem in this approach is Tomorrow, if two programmers are collaborating in a single project Then your other programmer might treat the age as a string instead of a number So, this can cause issues in runtime Typed dictionary tries to solve this problem With the help of typed dictionary, you define how your dictionary will look So, you first create a class In which you specify the name of the class And the name of the dictionary will be a key And the value of the key will be a string And the value of the age will be an integer And then you form your dictionaries with the help of this class So, tomorrow, if I make a dictionary of this class Then my code editor will tell me that the value of the name key should be a string And the value of the age key should be an integer So, this is to communicate the data type of the different keys of your dictionary Why should you use typed dictionary? It tells Python what keys are required and what data types of values they should have There is only one problem If you don't believe what is being told to you Suppose you are told that the age is an integer But you can still keep the value of the age as a string No one will stop you Your code will work So, there is no validation You have to remember that typed dictionary only tells you that the value of the data type should be an integer But if you make it a string, it doesn't stop you No one generates an error So, typed dictionary is just a way to define a dictionary Which will tell you the code editor Which value of which data type should be an integer I will quickly code it so that you understand So, what you have to do From typing, you have to import typed dictionary Now, we are making a class called person And this person will inherit from typed dictionary Now, you can tell the keys of your dictionary One key will be the name And we know that it should be a string Next key will be the age And we know that it should be an integer And that's it guys We have created a definition of our dictionary Now, suppose you want to make a new dictionary on this format Suppose, our new dictionary is named new person To make it, you use this syntax That new person is a type of person dictionary And here you specify inside a dictionary What should be the name Now, if you hover over the name It will tell you that name is a key Which should be of string type So, if tomorrow another programmer is using this He knows that in this particular dictionary Name should be a key Name should be a string Similarly, if I write age here If I hover over it again It will tell me that age should be an integer Okay And now, I can simply Print new person Save If I run this code You would see that your dictionary is not that special But if you actually do 35 tomorrow Then no one will stop you This code will still work See, this code still works So, that is the main idea behind typed dictionary I hope you understood this Now, I will tell you how to use typed dictionary How to create a data format And how to generate structured output Okay So, what we will do is We are taking a small problem statement We have some reviews Of phone And we will send these reviews to our LLM And we want to get a structured output And basically we need a dictionary In which one key will be summary Where we want That the summary of the review is generated And second We want there to be a sentiment Key where sentiment is generated Positive, negative or neutral And once I get this dictionary Then I can get the summary from this dictionary And get the sentiment Okay, so I have to do this Right, so see what will happen First of all, we will make a file Let's name this file With Structured Output Underscore Typed Dict.py In this file, we will take some imports So, first of all we will write from Blank chain open AI Import Chat open AI From .env Import Load.env And from Typing Import Typed dictionary Let's call load.env And form a model Chat open AI Okay, now see What we are going to do here I will show you the normal flow How we normally work So, normally our flow will be That you basically Invoke the model Okay, and here you will send Your review So, I have a review written already Let's see, this is our review And This review We send This time We will get some result And we will get the result Content Attribute Okay, this is the flow Now, what you will do differently You will create a schema here For your data How will your data format look So, first of all you will create a class Let's name the class Review And it will Inherit from typed dict And here We have to create two attributes So, the first attribute will be Summary And we know that Summary will be string Second Will be sentiment And sentiment we know will be String Okay, now see Main step comes here What we will do model.with Structured output function And here we will Pass the schema review Which we have created a class And we will call it Structured model Okay, so This model has This schema definition And now we will invoke This structured model Instead of invoking the model Okay And that's it Whatever result will come we will print it Like this Let's run this code And you can see guys We have a dictionary You can see its type See this is our type Run it So, it says we are getting a dictionary And since it is a dictionary You can Systematically fetch things from it Like print, result, summary Print Result Sentiment Save Now run See this This is sentiment And we have generated A separate summary So you can see how well structured Everything is happening Now I will explain to you what is happening How this is working It seems like magic because Nobody has written That you generate a summary Or a sentiment Still my model is understanding So what happens is When you call this With structured output function And provide a structure So behind the scene A system prompt is generated Which looks like this I have written it here Now exactly this doesn't happen But you can roughly understand You are an AI assistant That extracts structured insights from text Given a product review Extract, summary A brief overview of the main points And sentiment, overall tone Of the review Positive, neutral, negative Return the response in JSON format So this type of system Not exactly this But this type of prompt is generated And then this review is attached So this overall prompt is going to your LLM And since your LLM is trained To return JSON output It is giving you a JSON output Which looks like a dictionary in Python So this is how this entire Thing is working And it is very beautiful, very simple for us But behind the scenes I am pretty sure A lot of engineering will be involved Now let me show you a little more What is flexibility When you are making a scheme With the help of type dict What can be done So first of all you can do this In fact what I will do is I have decided a flow I will tell you according to that flow So I have told you the simple one Now I will show you the annotated one So what is happening so far We are simply asking it to generate Summary and sentiment Now our LLM is so well trained On data that it has understood That it has to generate summary And sentiment means it has understood That sentiment has to be generated So sometimes there is a little ambiguity Meaning it does not understand By reading a single word That it should generate a summary So here you can do You can guide your LLM You can attach a line Which it will understand by reading What to do So we call this annotation So for this you have to do By typing annotated You have to import And After that you can create Annotated definition Basically you can write The whole thing like this Here you can add a description A brief Summary Of the Review So now not only this is going on LLM has to send it a string It is also going on That What is the description So in every sentiment You can send this in annotated Return Sentiment Of the review Ok Here Negative Positive Or neutral Ok I did this because I don't want to take a chance I want This whole information goes to LLM And it should never be doubtful Ok so this is the funda of sending annotated Ok Now let's do one thing Let's try to parse a more complex Review So suppose I have this review A very big review And here I just don't want to generate Summary or sentiment I I want to extract all the topics discussed in this review Charging Processor I want to extract all the key ideas themes Key themes will be List because Multiple themes can be there so this will be list of string Ok This will be like a list of string Write down All the Key themes Discussed In the Review In a list Ok I have guided this Other than that Summary is done And sentiment is done Next thing is Ok there will be no comma here Next thing is pros Ok here It should be closed Next thing is pros Ok Pros And this is how Structured output You can extract from your LLM This method works right There is only one problem That There is no guarantee That if you say here that summary Should be a string Then it will come back as string Ok it is possible that Your LLM makes a mistake And it will give you a response In some other data format Or it is possible that You said that The only thing that you can show is the rating When it is more than 3 But it is possible that your LLM Will show you the response So if you want to put any data validation Then you can not put it here This is only for representation purpose Typed dictionary If you want to do data validation Then we have another option for this Which we call Pydantic And next we will discuss that Now with the help of Pydantic Before understanding this Let's understand what is Pydantic It is possible that you have not taught about Pydantic So Pydantic is a data validation And data parsing library For python It ensures that the data You work with is correct Structured and type safe So in simple words If you want to do data validation In any of your projects Means the data that is coming to you You want to put certain checks on it And if it is fulfilling some criteria Then you can use Pydantic And that is why when you Use fast API to build your APIs Then you use Pydantic Because while making APIs It is very important that Whatever kind of data you are processing It is in the right type and format Because then Safety matters a lot during API design So Pydantic Is super useful If you have not read it yet Then you should read it So what I will do is I will give you a small introduction Of Pydantic and how it works And then we will bring it in our workflow We will use it with structured output function So the good thing is You have read the typed dictionary You will see a very similar Syntax It is just that Pydantic is a little more powerful And can work a little more So what we will do is I will give you a very basic example And then we will make it More complex So what I have to do here I have to make a Maybe a dictionary And in that dictionary For now I have to store the name of a student And I have to make sure That the name of that student Is always a string There can be no other data format Okay So what you will do is First of all you have to Install Pydantic So you will write PIP install what you will do first From typing You will import optional Okay And here What I will do is I am making an attribute Saying age Which is an integer And not only integer It is an optional integer Okay, means It can be optional You just have to tell here That if That value is not set Meaning if you are not given age value Then its value will be none Means there is some value Either it will be none or interesting thing About Pyntic It tries to understand What data you have sent To it And if it can do it Then it does implicit data conversion Which we call type coarsing In Python So what I am saying is I have said here Age should be an integer But what if Age 32 came but in string format Now Pyntic Is smart enough to understand That this is a number But it came in string format But I want it in integer format So it will do an implicit type conversion And it will give you an integer 32 See if I run this code So you are still getting An integer value Ok You are getting this This is called type coarsing So whenever possible Pyntic does this work on its own It is smart enough After that There are certain things Which are present in Pyntic with which you can Perform some validation Like one syntax And what we have is Pyrantic object So if you want to fetch it You have to use this syntax Which you use in OP And if you run this Now it will give you the result If you want dictionary syntax Then you have to convert it in dictionary You can see this is the output If you want to see whole pyrantic object Then this is the output Here Same But This time we are using pyrantic So if something goes wrong Then we can perform validation here So this is more powerful than typed dictionary And most likely you will use it more In future So I hope you got to use With structured output function with pyrantic Next we will see How to do this whole work with the help of JSON So you use JSON schema When your whole project is not made in one language Means You have multiple languages And with the help of multiple languages You are building your project For example You are using python for your backend And you are using JavaScript for your frontend But at both the places You need JSON schema So here you will not use Pyrantic or typed dictionary Instead you will use JSON schema Because JSON is a universal Data format So I will show you How to create a schema in JSON So first I will show you A simple schema Of a student And then I will show you A schema of smartphone review So here I have made a file JSON file And what you have to do Is to make a schema Now in the schema You have to give 3-4 important things First, title of the schema This is what you have to tell Second, description This is optional But what we are doing Interacting with LLM Can be useful After that comes your type Where you tell the data type That the whole schema Will be of which data type After that comes the most important properties Here you write all your attributes And lastly, required Like it was optional Required does the same So suppose we wrote Student in title And In description we wrote Schema about students Your object will come in type Because It will look like a Python dictionary So in JSON It is called object After that in properties You can have multiple attributes So suppose our first attribute Is name So you will tell the type of name Name type is string Then age So age type is integer Like this And then finally comes required Where you write the name of those attributes Which you want That are included in your schema So suppose I said Name is required This is not going to work Age can be skipped But name should be there So this is what you can see on the screen This is a valid JSON schema So now I will show you How a full fledged Review of the Schema of the smartphone will look like So we will do one thing I have made a new file With structured output underscore JSON Here I am pasting the Pydantic code Like this Now what we have to do is The schema we made here This schema is written in Pydantic, we will remove it And instead we will put JSON schema here JSON schema looks a bit lengthy So I am not making it with full scratch I have written it I am directly copy pasting it And I will give you a walkthrough So see here Title is given Title is called review Type is object again Now see how many properties are there Key themes are properties Summary is a property Sentiment is a property Pros is a property Cons is a property And finally Name is a property Now see one by one Key themes if you know What we had planned is List of string Here I have told type is an array Array means list And all items inside it Type of all items will be string And here if you want You can give a description of Every attribute Similarly in summary Type will be string Here you don't use literal You use enum And in enum you have options Here description comes Pros is type Type is an array Or null Because it is an optional field And all items inside it Will be string Same for cons Name is either String or null Because it is optional And finally in required Key themes, summary and sentiment So it is the same You are writing it differently Now you don't have to do anything Just run this code Now the good thing is That here The object you get by flipping Is a python dictionary Just like type dict In pydantic there is a pydantic object In type dict there is a dictionary Here also there is a dictionary So it says name review is not defined Oh yeah, so what we have to do First of all This whole schema has to be stored In a variable, let's call it JSON schema And we will pass this schema Instead of this review class Here Like this And now we will run it And you can see You are getting the same output So now what we have learnt We learnt how to use With structured output And we have seen 3 formats 3 ways to make a schema First was Type dictionary Second was Pydantic And third was JSON schema We are solving The same problem with all three Now I will tell you In which scenario Which thing you should use So see you will use type dictionary When you Can use python in the whole project And you don't have to share Your schema in any other language With anyone Here it is written You only need type hints You just have to know which attribute And which data type And you don't need validation So you use type dictionary Generally it is less In real world scenarios You use Pydantic when you need Data validation Or you need to set some default value Because it is possible that LLM Will refuse to send any value Or you need an automatic type Conversion feature So in these scenarios you use Pydantic But again everything is happening In python universe And finally you use JSON schema You are not only using python universe You have other languages in the whole project And you want to explain them Your schema You don't want to import extra python libraries You need validation But you don't want to depend On python objects So JSON schema provides validation So here is a table You can go through it Which method provides you Basic structure All three provide Type enforcement Data validation is not in type dictionary Default values Not in type dictionary Not in JSON either Not in JSON I will cross check Automatic conversion is not in type dictionary And JSON And cross language compatibility Is not in type dictionary or Pydantic So since we are mostly Working in python universe We will focus more on Pydantic This will be our go to format Whenever we need to define schema Now guys you should know a couple of things Like when you Call with structured output function Then You can tell your model Which method you want structured output So what happens is With structured output function There is a method called parameter Okay Now in this you Can set two values One is JSON mode And second is Function calling Okay I will explain the difference between these two So you use JSON mode When you want structured output In JSON format Which is most of the times true And you use function calling When you want structured output You want it because You want to call a function By flipping the model So basically when you make Agents Where your agent calls a tool Like calculator So there you use Function calling format Okay so these two options are available With you If I tell you the rule of thumb If you are working with OpenAI models Then it is recommended to use function calling In fact this is default And if you are working with other models Like Claude Or Gemini Then you can use JSON mode Because these models Support JSON In structured output Then there are certain models Which do not support these two Basically they can't give structured output I will show you an example of this I had told you this before But I did not give you an example So the example is If you remember In the model lecture I had shown you This particular open source model Using hugging face Okay Now see what I am doing This whole code which we had written To extract JSON from review In that whole code What I will do is I am replacing chat openai With chat hugging face So instead of an openai model I am using this open source Tiny llama model And you will notice When you run this code And here you will call With structured output This code does not work This code errors through And the reason is that this tiny llama model Does not support structured output Neither in JSON mode Nor in function calling mode These two are not available here But you will have to apply output parsers On your own Which we will see in the next lecture So I hope you understood this So yeah guys With that I will conclude this video I hope what I wanted to convey In this whole video You understood it And you understood structured output Which is a very powerful concept Now in the next video We will discuss about output parsers So if you liked the video And if you have not subscribed this channel Please subscribe See you in the next video Bye

chunks.text: Hi guys, my name is Nitesh and you are welcome to my YouTube channel
In this video, we are going to continue our language chain playlist
and today's video is on a very interesting topic
and the name of this topic is Structured Output
Before starting the video, I would like to give you a little introduction about this topic
So, if you have used any LLM or chat GPT software till date
then you must have noticed that the biggest feature of LLMs is that
you can talk to humans in natural language
But what if we can make LLMs talk to other machines or systems
For example, I want my LLM to talk to my database
or my LLM to talk directly to my APIs
So, we are going to learn this in today's video
and to watch today's video well, I want to give you a big motivation
In the future, when I will teach you about agents
which is a big buzzword in today's date
then you will find today's topic very useful
So, on the whole, very important topic
Please make sure you don't skip this and watch this video end to end
So guys, before starting the video, let's take a recap
So, in the previous video, we discussed prompts
What are prompts in the language chain
So, I told you that prompts are basically inputs that you give to LLMs
So, in a way, I can say that in the previous video, we learned to give input to LLMs
Now, in today's video, we will learn to work with the output coming from LLMs
In fact, in today's video and in the next video, we will learn to process the output coming from LLMs
So, that is the main goal of today's video
Now, the topic of the video is structured output
So, let me explain to you what is structured output
Now guys, before understanding structured output, let me tell you what is unstructured output
So, if you have talked to any LLM, let's say, you have talked to chat GPT
You must have noticed that you send a text input to chat GPT
As soon as this prompt goes to your LLM, your LLM processes that prompt and then generates a response
And this response, generally speaking, is also a text
Like, if I say what is the capital of India, then I will get a response that New Delhi is the capital of India
Now, since the response is in text format, that is why we can call it unstructured output
Because there is no structure in text, right?
That is why the textual output that you get, you call it unstructured output
So, generally, when you are talking to any LLM, then you should accept that the output you are getting is unstructured
But, what if we can get some structure from it?
Let's say you gave this prompt to your LLM
Generally, what will happen is that your LLM will give you a response like this
In the morning, visit Eiffel Tower
In the afternoon, visit a museum
Now, since this is text, this is unstructured
But, what if our model gives us some kind of output like this?
That it first of all gives us output in JSON format
And that output should be structured in such a way
So, in total, three items are being told to you
So, they are being stored in three dictionaries
In every dictionary, time is told and activity is told
In the second dictionary, time and activity is told
In the third one, time and activity is told
Because there is a structure in it
Now, this structured output can be very beneficial
I will tell you later where you can use this kind of structured output
But for now, let's see this definition
Not only in English, we don't need a response
We need a response in a data format
What is the benefit of this?
That you can integrate this LLM with other systems
In fact, now what I will do is
I will tell you some use cases
That where structured outputs can give you a lot of benefit
So, I will tell you three use cases
There are more use cases than this
So, the first use case is data extraction
You are making a system where you have to store the output from LLM somewhere
So, you can use the structured output
For example, you are making a job portal
Now, people upload their resume on your job portal
But what you want is that you extract some key information from this resume
Like what is the name of the candidate
And what was his last company
What marks were there in college
You want to store all this
So, what you can do here is
You can use the structured output very easily
The candidate will upload the resume
So, we will extract all the text from the resume
We will send this text to LLM
And LLM will extract all this information in JSON format
And then we will write code on this JSON information
And insert all this information into a database
And we will do this for all our candidates
So, this can be a very good use case
If you want to build APIs
People write reviews of products on Amazon
You must have noticed that reviews are very long
Which will take up a complete review
And will generate some key information from it
What is the topic of this review
So, if it is a phone review
You will extract all those topics
After that, what you have told in the reviews
You will store it in one place
What you have told about the cons
You will store it in one place
And what is the overall sentiment of that review
You will store it in one place
And then you can give this information to anyone
Since you have made an API using this
Using a service like Flask or Fast API
Now anyone can take the output of this API in the whole world
So, this can be a very good second use case
And the third use case is
I have told you about agents in the second video of this playlist
I told you that agents are chatbots on steroids
Chatbots can only talk to you
But agents can also do some work
So, I told you that agents need tools to do their work
Like we can make a maths based agent
So, that agent can have a tool called calculator
Which will perform any complex mathematical operation
Now if you will tell this agent to find the square root of 2
Then you cannot send this text directly to the calculator
Use structured output on the prompt that agent has
That you have to find the square root
Now if you send these two pieces of information to the calculator
Then calculator will do its work and give it to you
So, all these tools that you use with agents
All of them need structured output
They cannot work on textual data
So, in the future when I will teach you how to make agents
How we generate structured output from LLM
And how we send those structured output to tool
So, these are three very important use cases
But more use cases exist than trust
And I would recommend you to think about it
That where you can use this structured output
In fact, I would recommend you to write a good idea in the comments
So, if I summarize the discussion till now
Structured output is a very powerful technique
Basic funda is that till today we were using LLMs
LLMs used to send messages in reverse
And they used to understand each other's point
The only problem is that LLMs are sending textual output
That's why you were not able to connect LLMs with other systems
We were not able to integrate LLMs with such systems
So, with the help of structured output
We gave a structure to output from LLM
Now, since we have given this data format
Now, LLMs can talk with other systems
So, this is the main benefit of having structured output
Till now, LLMs were talking with humans
Now, LLMs can talk with other machines
And that is why I am repeating in this video
That this is a very important topic that you should learn
Before coding, I would like to clarify one thing
That you will get to see two types of LLMs
One type of LLMs that can generate structured output by default
They are trained in such a way that if you talk to them

You get a function to work with them in LLMs

You can use this function and easily tell your LLM

Now, there is a way for models that can't generate structured output by default
There is a way for them as well

So, here the definition is written
Typed dictionary is a way to define a dictionary in Python
Where you specify what keys and values should exist
It helps ensure that your dictionary follows a specific structure
Till now, you used to make a dictionary in Python
With the name of a person
So, you used to make a dictionary on the go
That this is the name of the person
This is the age of the person
And then you used to use it
But the problem in this approach is
Tomorrow, if two programmers are collaborating in a single project
Then your other programmer might treat the age as a string instead of a number
So, this can cause issues in runtime
Typed dictionary tries to solve this problem
With the help of typed dictionary, you define how your dictionary will look
So, you first create a class
In which you specify the name of the class
And the name of the dictionary will be a key
And the value of the key will be a string
And the value of the age will be an integer
And then you form your dictionaries with the help of this class
So, tomorrow, if I make a dictionary of this class
Then my code editor will tell me that the value of the name key should be a string
And the value of the age key should be an integer
So, this is to communicate the data type of the different keys of your dictionary
Why should you use typed dictionary?
It tells Python what keys are required and what data types of values they should have

Suppose you are told that the age is an integer

You have to remember that typed dictionary only tells you that the value of the data type should be an integer
